The compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of the Value from 2012 to 2028 is 3.30%. This indicates a steady increase over the 16-year period. The waterfall chart visualizes the incremental changes each year, showing how the Value accumulates over time, with significant increases in 2017, 2018**, and projected growth by 2028***.

metadata.reference_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

# Data
data = {
    'Year': ['2012', '2013', '2014', '2015', '2016', '2017', '2018**', '2028***'],
    'Value': [23.4, 26.0, 26.3, 26.4, 26.5, 28.6, 29.8, 39.4]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Calculate the changes
df['Change'] = df['Value'].diff()
df.loc[0, 'Change'] = df.loc[0, 'Value']

# Calculate positions for waterfall
df['Cumulative'] = df['Change'].cumsum() - df['Change']
df['Color'] = df['Change'].apply(lambda x: 'green' if x >= 0 else 'red')

# Plot
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10,6))

ax.bar(df['Year'], df['Change'], bottom=df['Cumulative'], color=df['Color'], edgecolor='black')

# Annotate CAGR
ax.text(len(df)-1, df['Value'].iloc[-1], 'CAGR: 3.30%', ha='right', va='bottom')

# Labels and title
ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Value (billion GBP)')
ax.set_title('Waterfall Chart of Value Changes from 2012 to 2028')

plt.xticks(rotation=45)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
years = np.array([2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020])
net_sales = np.array([1762.15, 2291.52, 2801.06, 3229.14, 3287.12, 3464.12, 3470.29, 2882.56])

# Calculate CAGR from 2013 to 2019
begin_value = net_sales[0]
end_value = net_sales[6]
n = 6
CAGR = ((end_value / begin_value) ** (1 / n)) - 1

# Forecast net sales for 2020
forecasted_net_sales_2020 = end_value * (1 + CAGR)

# Absolute difference
difference = forecasted_net_sales_2020 - net_sales[7]

# Create subplots
fig, axs = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(18, 5))

# Subplot 1: Net Sales from 2013 to 2019
axs[0].plot(years[:7], net_sales[:7], marker='o')
axs[0].set_title('Net Sales 2013-2019')
axs[0].set_xlabel('Year')
axs[0].set_ylabel('Net Sales (million U.S. dollars)')
axs[0].annotate(f"CAGR: {CAGR * 100:.2f}%", xy=(2016, net_sales[3]), xytext=(2014, net_sales[0] + 500),
                arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', shrink=0.05))

# Subplot 2: Actual vs Forecasted Net Sales for 2020
axs[1].plot(years, np.append(net_sales[:7], forecasted_net_sales_2020), marker='o', label='Forecasted')
axs[1].plot(years, net_sales, marker='o', label='Actual')
axs[1].set_title('Forecasted vs Actual Net Sales')
axs[1].set_xlabel('Year')
axs[1].set_ylabel('Net Sales (million U.S. dollars)')
axs[1].legend()
axs[1].annotate(f"Difference: {difference:.2f}", xy=(2020, net_sales[7]), xytext=(2015, forecasted_net_sales_2020),
                arrowprops=dict(facecolor='red', shrink=0.05))

# Subplot 3: Comparison of Actual and Forecasted Net Sales in 2020
axs[2].bar(['Forecasted 2020', 'Actual 2020'], [forecasted_net_sales_2020, net_sales[7]], color=['blue', 'orange'])
axs[2].set_title('2020 Net Sales Comparison')
axs[2].set_ylabel('Net Sales (million U.S. dollars)')
axs[2].annotate(f"Difference: {difference:.2f}", xy=(0.5, max(forecasted_net_sales_2020, net_sales[7]) - 200),
                xytext=(0.5, max(forecasted_net_sales_2020, net_sales[7]) - 600),
                ha='center', arrowprops=dict(facecolor='green', arrowstyle='-|>'))

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

The pie chart highlights the 'Too Little' category with a different color and annotates it with its z-score, emphasizing its prominence in the distribution.

metadata.reference_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
labels = ['Too Much', "Don't Know", 'Too Little', 'Right Amount']
sizes = [5, 16, 48, 31]

# Calculate mean and standard deviation
mean = sum(sizes) / len(sizes)
differences = [(x - mean) ** 2 for x in sizes]
variance = sum(differences) / len(sizes)
std_dev = variance ** 0.5

# Calculate z-score for 'Too Little' category
too_little_value = sizes[2]  # 48
z_score = (too_little_value - mean) / std_dev

# Prepare colors, highlighting 'Too Little'
colors = ['lightgray', 'lightgray', 'skyblue', 'lightgray']

# Create Pie Chart
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
wedges, texts, autotexts = ax.pie(sizes, labels=labels, colors=colors,
                                  autopct='%1.1f%%', startangle=140)

# Highlight 'Too Little' wedge and annotate
for i, p in enumerate(wedges):
    if labels[i] == 'Too Little':
        p.set_edgecolor('black')
        p.set_linewidth(2)
        # Annotate with z-score
        ang = (p.theta2 - p.theta1) / 2. + p.theta1
        y = np.sin(np.deg2rad(ang))
        x = np.cos(np.deg2rad(ang))
        horizontalalignment = {-1: 'right', 1: 'left'}[int(np.sign(x))]
        connectionstyle = f'angle,angleA=0,angleB={{}}'.format(ang)
        ax.annotate(f'Z-Score: {z_score:.2f}', xy=(x, y), xytext=(1.4 * np.sign(x), 1.4 * y),
                    horizontalalignment=horizontalalignment,
                    arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='-', connectionstyle=connectionstyle))

# Equal aspect ratio ensures that pie is drawn as a circle
ax.axis('equal')

plt.title('Distribution of Responses')

plt.show()

metadata.reference_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
total_market = [41, 41, 42, 42, 46, 37, 38, 40, 43]
domestic_market = [28, 29, 31, 31, 35, 37, 38, 40, 43]

# Calculate quartiles and IQR for Total Market
total_market_sorted = sorted(total_market)
Q1 = np.percentile(total_market_sorted, 25)
Q2 = np.median(total_market_sorted)
Q3 = np.percentile(total_market_sorted, 75)
IQR = Q3 - Q1

# Calculate outlier thresholds
lower_whisker = Q1 - 1.5 * IQR
upper_whisker = Q3 + 1.5 * IQR

# Identify outliers in Domestic Market when compared to Total Market
outliers = [value for value in domestic_market if value < lower_whisker or value > upper_whisker]

# Create box plot for Total Market
fig, ax = plt.subplots()
box = ax.boxplot(total_market, vert=True, positions=[1], widths=0.6, patch_artist=True, showfliers=False)

# Plot Domestic Market data points
for value in domestic_market:
    if value in outliers:
        ax.plot(1, value, 'ro')  # Outliers marked in red
    else:
        ax.plot(1, value, 'k.')  # Other data points in black

# Annotate median and quartiles
ax.annotate('Q1 = {:.1f}'.format(Q1), xy=(1.05, Q1), xytext=(1.1, Q1), fontsize=8)
ax.annotate('Median = {:.1f}'.format(Q2), xy=(1.05, Q2), xytext=(1.1, Q2), fontsize=8)
ax.annotate('Q3 = {:.1f}'.format(Q3), xy=(1.05, Q3), xytext=(1.1, Q3), fontsize=8)

# Annotate outliers
for value in outliers:
    ax.annotate('Outlier', xy=(1, value), xytext=(0.95, value), textcoords='data', fontsize=8, color='red')

# Set labels and title
ax.set_xlabel('Total Market')
ax.set_ylabel('Market Values')
ax.set_title('Box Plot of Total Market with Domestic Market Outliers')

plt.show()

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Data
data = {
    'Player': ['Hines Ward', 'Antonio Brown', 'John Stallworth', 'Heath Miller', 'Louis Lipps', 'Lynn Swann', 'Elbie Nickel', 'Buddy Dial', 'Plaxico Burress', 'Mike Wallace', 'Ray Mathews', 'Santonio Holmes', 'JuJu Smith-Schuster', 'Roy Jefferson', 'Yancey Thigpen', 'Charles Johnson', 'Ron Shanklin', 'Gary Ballman', 'Bennie Cunningham', 'Eric Green'],
    'ReceivingYards': [12083, 11207, 8723, 6569, 6018, 5462, 5131, 4723, 4206, 4042, 3919, 3835, 3726, 3671, 3651, 3400, 3047, 2949, 2879, 2681]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Handle missing values if present
df.dropna(inplace=True)

# Sort the data in descending order of ReceivingYards
df = df.sort_values(by='ReceivingYards', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

# Calculate cumulative sum and cumulative percentage
df['CumulativeYards'] = df['ReceivingYards'].cumsum()
total_yards = df['ReceivingYards'].sum()
df['CumulativePercentage'] = df['CumulativeYards'] / total_yards * 100

# Plotting
fig, axs = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(12, 10))

# Subplot 1: Bar chart of Receiving Yards
axs[0].bar(df['Player'], df['ReceivingYards'], color='skyblue')
axs[0].set_title('Receiving Yards per Player')
axs[0].set_xlabel('Player')
axs[0].set_ylabel('Receiving Yards')
axs[0].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=90)

# Annotate top contributor
axs[0].annotate('Top contributor', xy=(0, df['ReceivingYards'][0]), xytext=(1, df['ReceivingYards'][0]+1000),
                arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', arrowstyle='->'))

# Subplot 2: Pareto Chart
axs[1].bar(df['Player'], df['ReceivingYards'], color='skyblue')
axs2 = axs[1].twinx()
axs2.plot(df['Player'], df['CumulativePercentage'], color='red', marker='D', linestyle='-', linewidth=2)
axs[1].set_xlabel('Player')
axs[1].set_ylabel('Receiving Yards')

axs[1].tick_params(axis='x', rotation=90)
axs[1].set_title('Pareto Chart of Receiving Yards')

# Highlighting the point where cumulative percentage crosses 50%
cross_point = df[df['CumulativePercentage'] >= 50].index[0]
axs2.vlines(cross_point, ymin=0, ymax=df['CumulativePercentage'][cross_point], color='green', linestyles='dashed')

axs2.annotate('50% cumulative', xy=(cross_point, df['CumulativePercentage'][cross_point]),
              xytext=(cross_point+1, df['CumulativePercentage'][cross_point]+5),
              arrowprops=dict(facecolor='green', arrowstyle='->'))

plt.tight_layout()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    'Skin care': [4.41, 4.54, 4.63, 4.68, 4.73, 4.77, 4.82],

}

categories = list(data.keys())
values = list(data.values())

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

medianprops = dict(linestyle='-', linewidth=2, color='red')
flierprops = dict(marker='o', markerfacecolor='green', markersize=8, linestyle='none')

metadata.reference_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
t = np.array([0, 7, 13, 25])
bad_news = np.array([80, 31, 29, 24])

# Perform linear regression
coefficients = np.polyfit(t, bad_news, 1)
m = coefficients[0]
b = coefficients[1]

# Compute t when y=0
t_zero = -b / m

# Create t values for plotting regression line
t_line = np.linspace(0, t_zero, 100)
y_line = m * t_line + b

# Plot data points
plt.scatter(t, bad_news, color='blue', label='Data Points')

# Plot regression line
plt.plot(t_line, y_line, color='red', label='Regression Line')

# Highlight the point where y=0
plt.plot(t_zero, 0, 'go', label='Predicted Zero Point')

# Annotate the zero point
plt.annotate(f't = {t_zero:.1f}', (t_zero, 0), textcoords="offset points", xytext=(0,10), ha='center')

# Annotate the regression equation
regression_eq = f'y = {m:.2f}t + {b:.2f}'
plt.annotate(regression_eq, xy=(0.05, 0.85), xycoords='axes fraction')

# Text annotations
plt.title('Linear Regression of "Mostly Bad News" Percentage Over Time')
plt.xlabel('Months since Dec 2008')
plt.ylabel('Mostly bad news percentage')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)

# Show the plot
plt.show()

metadata.reference_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
company_sizes = ['Up to 100 employees', '100 to 500 employees', 'More than 500 employees']
turnover_categories = ['Less than 10%', '10 to 50%', 'More than 50%', 'Do not know/not applicable']

# Percentages
data = {
    'Up to 100 employees': [77, 12, 3, 8],
    '100 to 500 employees': [37, 53, 5, 5],
    'More than 500 employees': [23, 40, 31, 6]
}

# Midpoints for turnover ranges
midpoints = {'Less than 10%': 5, '10 to 50%': 30, 'More than 50%': 75}

# Compute estimated average turnover rates
estimated_averages = []

for company_size in company_sizes:
    percentages = data[company_size]
    known_percentages = percentages[:3]
    known_total = sum(known_percentages)
    # If known_total is zero, avoid division by zero
    if known_total == 0:
        estimated_average = 0
    else:
        # Compute numerator
        numerator = 0
        for i, category in enumerate(turnover_categories[:3]):
            numerator += (known_percentages[i] / 100.0) * midpoints[category]
        # Estimated average turnover rate
        estimated_average = numerator / (known_total / 100.0)
    estimated_averages.append(estimated_average)

# Setup subplots
fig, axs = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(12, 6))

# Subplot 1: Grouped bar chart of turnover categories
x = np.arange(len(company_sizes))  # Label locations
width = 0.2  # Width of the bars

for i, category in enumerate(turnover_categories[:3]):
    percentages = [data[company_size][i] for company_size in company_sizes]
    axs[0].bar(x + i*width - width, percentages, width, label=category)

axs[0].set_xlabel('Company Size')
axs[0].set_ylabel('Percentage (%)')
axs[0].set_title('Distribution of Turnover Rates by Company Size')
axs[0].set_xticks(x)
axs[0].set_xticklabels(company_sizes)
axs[0].legend()
axs[0].annotate('Highest "More than 50%" in largest companies', xy=(2,31), xytext=(1.5,35),
                arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', shrink=0.05))

# Subplot 2: Bar chart of estimated average turnover rates
axs[1].bar(company_sizes, estimated_averages, color='orange')
axs[1].set_xlabel('Company Size')
axs[1].set_ylabel('Estimated Average Turnover Rate (%)')
axs[1].set_title('Estimated Average Turnover Rate by Company Size')
axs[1].annotate('Highest average turnover rate', xy=(2, estimated_averages[2]), 
                xytext=(1.5, estimated_averages[2]+5),
                arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', shrink=0.05))

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
from sklearn.linear_model import LinearRegression
import numpy as np

# Data
data = {
    'Year': [2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020],
    'ACSI_score': [77, 78, 77, 76, 78, 80, 76, 80, 76, 74, 75, 77, 78, 79, 78]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Handle missing values if any
df = df.dropna()

# Plotting
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 10))

# Subplot 1: ACSI scores over time
plt.subplot(2, 2, 1)
plt.plot(df['Year'], df['ACSI_score'], marker='o')
plt.title('ACSI Scores Over Time')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('ACSI Score')
plt.grid(True)
# Annotation
max_score_year = df.loc[df['ACSI_score'].idxmax(), 'Year']
max_score = df['ACSI_score'].max()
plt.annotate(f'Highest Score: {max_score}', xy=(max_score_year, max_score), xytext=(max_score_year, max_score + 1),
             arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', shrink=0.05))

# Subplot 2: ACSI scores and 3-year moving average
df['Moving_Average'] = df['ACSI_score'].rolling(window=3).mean()
plt.subplot(2, 2, 2)
plt.plot(df['Year'], df['ACSI_score'], label='ACSI Score', marker='o')
plt.plot(df['Year'], df['Moving_Average'], label='3-Year Moving Average', linestyle='--')
plt.title('ACSI Scores and 3-Year Moving Average')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('ACSI Score')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
# Annotation
significant_deviation = df[abs(df['ACSI_score'] - df['Moving_Average']) > 2]
for _, row in significant_deviation.iterrows():
    plt.annotate('Significant Deviation', xy=(row['Year'], row['ACSI_score']), xytext=(row['Year'], row['ACSI_score'] + 2),
                 arrowprops=dict(facecolor='red', shrink=0.05))

# Subplot 3: Year-over-Year Percentage Change
df['YoY_Percentage_Change'] = df['ACSI_score'].pct_change() * 100
plt.subplot(2, 2, 3)
plt.bar(df['Year'], df['YoY_Percentage_Change'])
plt.title('Year-over-Year Percentage Change')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Percentage Change (%)')
plt.grid(True)
# Annotation
max_increase_idx = df['YoY_Percentage_Change'].idxmax()
max_increase_year = df.loc[max_increase_idx, 'Year']
max_increase = df.loc[max_increase_idx, 'YoY_Percentage_Change']
plt.annotate(f'Highest Increase: {max_increase:.2f}%', xy=(max_increase_year, max_increase), xytext=(max_increase_year, max_increase + 1),
             arrowprops=dict(facecolor='green', shrink=0.05))
max_decrease_idx = df['YoY_Percentage_Change'].idxmin()
max_decrease_year = df.loc[max_decrease_idx, 'Year']
max_decrease = df.loc[max_decrease_idx, 'YoY_Percentage_Change']
plt.annotate(f'Highest Decrease: {max_decrease:.2f}%', xy=(max_decrease_year, max_decrease), xytext=(max_decrease_year, max_decrease - 5),
             arrowprops=dict(facecolor='red', shrink=0.05))

# Subplot 4: Linear Regression and Prediction
X = df['Year'].values.reshape(-1, 1)
y = df['ACSI_score'].values

model = LinearRegression()
model.fit(X, y)
pred_year = np.array([[2021]])
predicted_score = model.predict(pred_year)[0]

# Plotting the regression line
plt.subplot(2, 2, 4)
plt.scatter(df['Year'], df['ACSI_score'], color='blue', label='Actual Data')
plt.plot(df['Year'], model.predict(X), color='red', label='Regression Line')
plt.scatter(2021, predicted_score, color='green', marker='X', label=f'Predicted 2021 Score: {predicted_score:.2f}')
plt.title('Linear Regression Prediction')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('ACSI Score')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
# Annotation
plt.annotate(f'Predicted Score: {predicted_score:.2f}', xy=(2021, predicted_score), xytext=(2018, predicted_score + 1),
             arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', shrink=0.05))

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
years = [2004, 2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018]
revenue = [0.83, 1.13, 1.62, 2.38, 2.9, 3.44, 4.32, 4.98, 4.79, 5.5, 5.9, 7.3, 7.88, 8.49, 9.8]

# Calculate annual percentage increase
percentage_increase = [0]
for i in range(1, len(revenue)):
    increase = ((revenue[i] - revenue[i-1]) / revenue[i-1]) * 100
    percentage_increase.append(increase)

# Calculate log of revenue
log_revenue = np.log(revenue)

# Plotting
fig, axs = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(10, 15))

# Subplot 1: Revenue over time
axs[0].plot(years, revenue, marker='o')
axs[0].set_title('Revenue over Time')
axs[0].set_xlabel('Year')
axs[0].set_ylabel('Revenue (billion euros)')
axs[0].annotate('Highest revenue in 2018', xy=(2018, 9.8), xytext=(2010, 8), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->'))

# Subplot 2: Annual Percentage Increase
axs[1].bar(years, percentage_increase)
axs[1].set_title('Annual Percentage Increase in Revenue')
axs[1].set_xlabel('Year')
axs[1].set_ylabel('Percentage Increase (%)')
axs[1].annotate('Highest increase in 2007', xy=(2007, percentage_increase[3]), xytext=(2009, 40), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->'))

# Subplot 3: Log of Revenue over time
axs[2].plot(years, log_revenue, marker='o')
axs[2].set_title('Log of Revenue over Time')
axs[2].set_xlabel('Year')
axs[2].set_ylabel('Log(Revenue)')
axs[2].annotate('Linear trend suggests exponential growth', xy=(2016, log_revenue[12]), xytext=(2006, 1), arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->'))

plt.show()

rects1 = ax.bar(x - width/2, offline_sales, width, label='Offline Sales')
rects2 = ax.bar(x + width/2, online_sales, width, label='Online Sales')

# Annotate percentages on top of bars

    for rect in rects:

        ax.annotate('{:.1f}%'.format(height),
                    xy=(rect.get_x() + rect.get_width() / 2, height),
                    xytext=(0, 3),  # 3 points vertical offset

metadata.reference_code:
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
elements = ['Coal', 'Natural Gas', 'Nuclear', 'Hydro', 'Wind', 'Solar']
values = [2800, 2600, 2200, 1800, 1600, 1000]

# Colors
colors = ['#7f8c8d', '#2ecc71', '#3498db', '#9b59b6', '#f1c40f', '#e74c3c']

# Create figure and axis
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8,8))

# Create pie chart with hole in the center
wedges, texts, autotexts = ax.pie(values, labels=elements, colors=colors,
                                  autopct='%1.1f%%', startangle=140,
                                  pctdistance=0.85, labeldistance=1.05,
                                  wedgeprops={'width':0.3, 'edgecolor':'white'})

# Draw circle in the center to make it donut-shaped
centre_circle = plt.Circle((0,0),0.70,fc='white')
fig.gca().add_artist(centre_circle)

# Equal aspect ratio ensures that pie is drawn as a circle
ax.axis('equal')

# Annotations
total_energy = sum(values)
percentages = [v / total_energy * 100 for v in values]

# Highlight the slices in question
highlight_indices = [elements.index('Nuclear'), elements.index('Wind')]
for i in highlight_indices:
    wedges[i].set_edgecolor('black')
    wedges[i].set_linewidth(2)
    # Annotate the wedge
    ang = (wedges[i].theta2 + wedges[i].theta1)/2.
    y = np.sin(np.deg2rad(ang))
    x = np.cos(np.deg2rad(ang))
    horizontalalignment = {-1: "right", 1: "left"}[int(np.sign(x))]
    connectionstyle = "angle,angleA=0,angleB={}".format(ang)
    ax.annotate(f"{elements[i]} ({values[i]} TWh)", xy=(x, y), xytext=(1.35*np.sign(x), 1.4*y),
                horizontalalignment=horizontalalignment, arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle="-", connectionstyle=connectionstyle))

# Add title
ax.set_title('Energy Production by Source')

# Show plot
plt.show()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

species = ['Fin whale', 'Sperm whale', 'Humpback whale', 'Minke whale', 'Sei whale', 'Blue whale', 'Bryde's whale', 'Gray whale', 'Right whale']
kills = [30693, 20549, 8560, 5276, 4727, 1383, 29, 8, 3]

average_kills = sum(kills) / len(kills)

differences = [abs(kill - average_kills) for kill in kills]
min_diff_index = differences.index(min(differences))
closest_species = species[min_diff_index]

# Colors: highlight the bar for the closest species
colors = ['skyblue'] * len(species)
colors[min_diff_index] = 'orange'  # Humpback whale

# Rotate x-axis labels for better fit

)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Price data for each item over the years 2010/11 to 2014/15
hot_dog_prices = [4.14, 4.19, 4.48, 4.74, 4.84]
soft_drink_prices = [3.90, 3.91, 4.06, 4.39, 4.46]
beer_prices = [6.71, 6.80, 7.07, 7.34, 7.54]

data = [hot_dog_prices, soft_drink_prices, beer_prices]
labels = ['Hot dog', 'Soft drink', 'Beer']

fig, ax = plt.subplots()

# Plot the boxplot
bp = ax.boxplot(data, labels=labels, showmeans=True, meanline=True)

# Function to calculate quartiles and quartile skewness
def calculate_quartiles(dataset):
    q1 = np.percentile(dataset, 25)
    median = np.median(dataset)
    q3 = np.percentile(dataset, 75)
    return q1, median, q3

def quartile_skewness(q1, median, q3):
    return (q3 + q1 - 2 * median) / (q3 - q1)

# Calculate quartiles and skewness for each item
hot_dog_q1, hot_dog_median, hot_dog_q3 = calculate_quartiles(hot_dog_prices)
hot_dog_skewness = quartile_skewness(hot_dog_q1, hot_dog_median, hot_dog_q3)

soft_drink_q1, soft_drink_median, soft_drink_q3 = calculate_quartiles(soft_drink_prices)
soft_drink_skewness = quartile_skewness(soft_drink_q1, soft_drink_median, soft_drink_q3)

beer_q1, beer_median, beer_q3 = calculate_quartiles(beer_prices)
beer_skewness = quartile_skewness(beer_q1, beer_median, beer_q3)

# Positions for the labels
positions = np.arange(1, len(labels) + 1)

# Skewness values
skewness_values = [hot_dog_skewness, soft_drink_skewness, beer_skewness]

# Annotate skewness on the plot
for pos, skew in zip(positions, skewness_values):
    ax.text(pos, max(data[pos - 1]) + 0.1, f'Skewness: {skew:.3f}', ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=8)

ax.set_xlabel('Item')

ax.set_title('Price Distribution of Items from 2010/11 to 2014/15')

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
years = [1980, 1985, 1990, 1995, 2000, 2005, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018]
expenditures = [374.35, 438.34, 474.65, 514.62, 687.71, 1045.73, 1214.05, 1391.71, 1355.03, 1376.14, 1394.93, 1128.07, 1038.27, 1136.5, 1271.06]

# Calculate cumulative expenditures
cumulative_expenditures = np.cumsum(expenditures)

# Total cumulative expenditure
total_cumulative_expenditure = cumulative_expenditures[-1]

# Half of the total cumulative expenditure
half_total = total_cumulative_expenditure / 2

# Find the year when cumulative expenditure first exceeds half of total
for i, cum_exp in enumerate(cumulative_expenditures):
    if cum_exp >= half_total:
        half_year = years[i]
        break

# Plot area chart
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
plt.fill_between(years, expenditures, color='skyblue', alpha=0.5)
plt.plot(years, expenditures, color='slateblue', alpha=0.7)

# Annotate the point where cumulative expenditure reaches half of total
plt.axvline(x=half_year, color='red', linestyle='--', label=f'Cumulative expenditure reaches half at {half_year}')
plt.legend()
plt.title('Expenditure over Years')
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Expenditure in billion U.S. dollars')

# Annotate the point on the curve
plt.annotate(f'Half total cumulative expenditure reached in {half_year}',
             xy=(half_year, expenditures[years.index(half_year)]),
             xytext=(half_year+2, expenditures[years.index(half_year)]+100),
             arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', arrowstyle='->'),
             fontsize=10)

plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

x = [1, 2, 3, 4]

mean_y = np.mean(y)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

data = {
    'Year': [2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019],
    'Expenditure': [5225, 5179, 5057, 4370, 4298, 5087, 4686, 4583, 4924, 5696, 5621, 6537, 6458, 6582, 6547]
}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

df['Change'] = df['Expenditure'].diff().fillna(df['Expenditure'])

df['Cumulative'] = df['Change'].cumsum().shift(1).fillna(0) + df['Expenditure'][0]

df['Positive'] = df['Change'].apply(lambda x: x if x >= 0 else 0)
df['Negative'] = df['Change'].apply(lambda x: x if x < 0 else 0)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12, 6))

ax.bar(df['Year'], df['Positive'], bottom=df['Cumulative'] - df['Positive'], color='green', label='Increase')
ax.bar(df['Year'], df['Negative'], bottom=df['Cumulative'] - df['Negative'], color='red', label='Decrease')

ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Expenditure in million GBP')
ax.set_title('Waterfall Chart of Year-over-Year Changes in Expenditure (2005-2019)')
ax.legend()

total_decrease = int(df['Negative'].sum())
ax.text(df['Year'].min(), df['Expenditure'].max(), f'Total Decrease: {total_decrease} million GBP', color='red')

    'Company': ['UniFeeder', 'Emirates Shipping', 'Global Feeder LLC', 'SeaLeadShipping', 'Far Shipping', 'Westwood', 'Peel Ports', 'Independent Container', 'Melfi Marine', 'King Ocean', 'Bengal Tiger Line', 'Nirint Lines', 'Rizhao Haitong Ferry', 'Asean Seas Line', 'Pendulum Express Line', 'Zim', 'Boluda Lines', 'Caribbean Feeder Services', 'Seaboard Marine', 'Del Monte Fresh Produce', 'DAL'],

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
years = [2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020]
shares = [2268, 3095, 3071, 3041, 2963, 2894, 2816, 2766, 2730, 2664, 2565, 2530]

# Calculate annual changes
changes = [shares[0]] + [shares[i] - shares[i-1] for i in range(1, len(shares))]

# Labels for the waterfall chart
labels = ['2009 (Start)'] + [str(year) for year in years[1:]]

# Create figure and axes
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12,6))

# Waterfall plotting
def plot_waterfall(ax, labels, data):
    cum_data = np.cumsum(data)
    blank = cum_data - data
    colors = ['#4CAF50' if x >= 0 else '#F44336' for x in data]
    ax.bar(labels, data, bottom=blank, color=colors)

    # Annotate largest decrease
    min_change = min(data[1:])  # exclude the initial value
    min_index = data[1:].index(min_change) + 1
    ax.annotate(f'Largest Decrease: {min_change} million',
                xy=(labels[min_index], cum_data[min_index]),
                xytext=(labels[min_index], cum_data[min_index]+200),
                arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', shrink=0.05),
                ha='center')

    ax.set_xlabel('Year')
    ax.set_ylabel('Number of Shares (in millions)')
    ax.set_title('Annual Changes in Number of Shares from 2009 to 2020')
    plt.xticks(rotation=45)
    plt.tight_layout()
    plt.show()

plot_waterfall(ax, labels, data=changes)

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Data
years = [2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020]
production = [8108, 8425, 9196, 9242, 9871, 10684, 10649, 11225, 11026, 10959, 11546, 11897, 12186, 12187, 11741]

# Calculate year-over-year changes
yoy_changes = [production[0]]  # Starting point
for i in range(1, len(production)):
    yoy_changes.append(production[i] - production[i - 1])

# Prepare cumulative data
cumulative = [production[0]]
for i in range(1, len(yoy_changes)):
    cumulative.append(cumulative[i - 1] + yoy_changes[i])

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(16, 6))

# Color logic
bar_colors = ['blue'] + ['green' if change >= 0 else 'red' for change in yoy_changes[1:]]

# Plot bars
ax.bar(range(len(yoy_changes)), yoy_changes, bottom=[0] + cumulative[:-1], color=bar_colors, edgecolor='black')

# X-axis labels
ax.set_xticks(range(len(yoy_changes)))
ax.set_xticklabels([str(year) for year in years], rotation=45)

# Labels and Title
ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Production (thousand metric tons)')
ax.set_title('Waterfall Chart of Year-over-Year Production Changes', pad=40)

# Adjusted Annotation for Net Change (moved down)
net_change = cumulative[-1] - cumulative[0]
ax.text(len(cumulative)/2, max(cumulative) - 300, f'Net change: {int(net_change)} thousand metric tons',
        ha='center', va='bottom', fontsize=12, bbox=dict(facecolor='white', alpha=0.7))

# Gridlines for clarity
ax.yaxis.grid(True, linestyle='--', alpha=0.7)

# Legend

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
from io import StringIO
from scipy.stats import linregress

# Data provided as a multi-line string
data = '''Year,Should keep out,Should express views
68.0,53.0,0.0
nan,0.0,0.0
80.0,0.0,0.0
86.0,0.0,0.0
92.0,0.0,0.0
98.0,43.0,0.0
0.0,0.0,0.0
10.0,52.0,0.0'''

# Read the data into a DataFrame
df = pd.read_csv(StringIO(data))

# Clean and preprocess data
df = df.dropna()
df = df[df['Year'] != 0.0]
# Convert 'Year' to integer and adjust for years less than 100
df['Year'] = df['Year'].apply(lambda x: int(1900 + x) if x < 100 else int(x))
# Filter out zero response rates
df_nonzero = df[df['Should keep out'] != 0.0]

# Perform linear regression
slope, intercept, r_value, p_value, std_err = linregress(df_nonzero['Year'], df_nonzero['Should keep out'])

# Predict the response rate for 2020
year = 2020
projected_rate = intercept + slope * year
projected_rate = round(projected_rate, 2)

# Plot the data
plt.figure(figsize=(10,6))
plt.plot(df_nonzero['Year'], df_nonzero['Should keep out'], 'o', label='Historical Data')
plt.plot(df_nonzero['Year'], intercept + slope * df_nonzero['Year'], 'r--', label='Trend Line')
plt.plot(year, projected_rate, 'go', label=f'2020 Projection: {projected_rate}')
plt.annotate(f'Projection: {projected_rate}', xy=(year, projected_rate), xytext=(year-10, projected_rate+5),
             arrowprops=dict(facecolor='green', arrowstyle='->'), fontsize=12)

plt.title("Projected 'Should keep out' Response Rate for 2020")
plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Should keep out')
plt.legend()
plt.grid(True)
plt.show()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
entities = ['Dem/Lean Dem', 'Rep/Lean Rep', 'Total']
mostly_bad = np.array([68, 34, 51])
mostly_good = np.array([np.nan, 53.0, 35.0])

# Calculate missing percentages
missing = []
for mbad, mgood in zip(mostly_bad, mostly_good):
    total = mbad + (mgood if not np.isnan(mgood) else 0)
    missing_percentage = 100 - total
    missing.append(missing_percentage)

# Set up the figure and axes
fig, axs = plt.subplots(1, 3, figsize=(15, 5))

# Subplot 1: Bar chart of 'Mostly bad' percentages
axs[0].bar(entities, mostly_bad, color='red')
axs[0].set_title("'Mostly bad' percentages")

axs[0].set_ylabel('Percentage')
for i, v in enumerate(mostly_bad):
    axs[0].text(i, v + 1, str(v) + '%', ha='center')

# Subplot 2: Bar chart of 'Mostly good' percentages
axs[1].bar(entities, [0 if np.isnan(v) else v for v in mostly_good], color='green')
axs[1].set_title("'Mostly good' percentages")
axs[1].set_xlabel('Entity')
for i, v in enumerate(mostly_good):
    value = 0 if np.isnan(v) else v

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

# Data
agency_counts = [1302, 1341, 1368, 1371, 1447, 1486, 1515]
all_other_counts = [159, 166, 186, 189, 196, 201, 217]
finance_counts = [7, 7, 8, 10, 11, 11, 12]

data = [agency_counts, all_other_counts, finance_counts]
categories = ['Agency', 'All Other', 'Finance']

# Calculate statistics for annotations
def calculate_stats(data):
    stats = {}
    stats['median'] = np.median(data)
    stats['q1'] = np.percentile(data, 25)
    stats['q3'] = np.percentile(data, 75)
    stats['iqr'] = stats['q3'] - stats['q1']
    stats['mean'] = np.mean(data)
    stats['std'] = np.std(data, ddof=1)
    stats['skewness'] = 3 * (stats['mean'] - stats['median']) / stats['std'] if stats['std'] != 0 else 0
    return stats

agency_stats = calculate_stats(agency_counts)
all_other_stats = calculate_stats(all_other_counts)
finance_stats = calculate_stats(finance_counts)

stats_list = [agency_stats, all_other_stats, finance_stats]

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(10, 6))

# Create box plot
bp = ax.boxplot(data, labels=categories, showmeans=True, meanline=True, patch_artist=True, notch=True)

# Set colors
colors = ['lightblue', 'lightgreen', 'lightcoral']
for patch, color in zip(bp['boxes'], colors):
    patch.set_facecolor(color)

# Annotate median, quartiles, and skewness
for i, stats in enumerate(stats_list):
    x_pos = i + 1
    ax.annotate(f"Median: {stats['median']:.2f}", xy=(x_pos + 0.1, stats['median']))
    ax.annotate(f"Q1: {stats['q1']:.2f}", xy=(x_pos + 0.1, stats['q1']))
    ax.annotate(f"Q3: {stats['q3']:.2f}", xy=(x_pos + 0.1, stats['q3']))
    ax.annotate(f"Skewness: {stats['skewness']:.2f}", xy=(x_pos + 0.1, stats['q3'] + (stats['iqr'] / 2)))

# Highlight outliers
for flier in bp['fliers']:
    ydata = flier.get_ydata()
    xdata = flier.get_xdata()
    ax.plot(xdata, ydata, 'ro')
    for x, y in zip(xdata, ydata):
        ax.annotate('Outlier', xy=(x + 0.1, y))

ax.set_xlabel('Category')
ax.set_ylabel('Counts')
ax.set_title('Box Plot of Counts by Category (2012-2018)')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Data
data = {'Sector': ['Real estate activities', 'Health and social work', 'Wholesale, retail, repair of vehicles', 'Manufacturing', 'Prof, scientific, technical activ.', 'Information and communication', 'Financial and insurance activities', 'Education', 'Construction', 'Public admin and defence', 'Admin and support services', 'Transport and storage', 'Accommodation and food services', 'Electricity, gas, air cond supply', 'Water supply, sewerage, waste', 'Other service activities', 'Arts, entertainment and recreation', 'Mining and quarrying', 'Agriculture, forestry and fishing', 'Households as employers'],
        'GVA': [262786, 194622, 189877, 173883, 150864, 131521, 124964, 122768, 112382, 107083, 84803, 68288, 27083, 27048, 24490, 24101, 21188, 13397, 11752, 4570]}

df = pd.DataFrame(data)

# Handle missing values if present
df = df.dropna()

# Sort data by GVA descending
df = df.sort_values(by='GVA', ascending=False).reset_index(drop=True)

# Calculate cumulative GVA and cumulative percentage
df['Cumulative GVA'] = df['GVA'].cumsum()
total_GVA = df['GVA'].sum()
df['Cumulative Percentage'] = df['Cumulative GVA'] / total_GVA * 100

# Plotting
fig, ax = plt.subplots(2, 1, figsize=(12, 10))

# Subplot 1: Bar chart of GVA by sector
ax[0].bar(df['Sector'], df['GVA'], color='skyblue')

# Subplot 2: Pareto chart
ax2 = ax[1]
ax2.bar(df['Sector'], df['GVA'], color='skyblue')

ax3.set_ylabel('Cumulative Percentage')

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

# Data
years = [2007, 2008, 2009, 2010, 2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2017, 2018, 2019, 2020]
sales = [67.0, 56.0, 56.0, 56.0, 57.4, 57.4, 61.9, 74.0, 77.0, 82.8, 83.2, 85.8, 92.0, 135.4]

# Calculate incremental changes
changes = [sales[0]]  # Starting value
deltas = [sales[0]] + [sales[i] - sales[i-1] for i in range(1, len(sales))]
labels = ['Initial'] + [str(year) for year in years[1:]]

# Calculate running total for waterfall
from itertools import accumulate
running_total = list(accumulate(deltas))

# Prepare for plotting
blank = running_total.copy()
blank.insert(0, 0)
blank.pop()

colors = ['blue'] + ['green' if x >= 0 else 'red' for x in deltas[1:]]

# Plotting
fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12,6))

for i in range(len(deltas)):
    ax.bar(labels[i], deltas[i], bottom=blank[i], color=colors[i], edgecolor='black')
    y = blank[i] + deltas[i]/2
    ax.text(i, y, f'{deltas[i]:.1f}', ha='center', va='center', color='white')

# Annotate net change
net_change = sales[-1] - sales[0]
ax.annotate(f'Net Change: {net_change:.1f} million', xy=(len(labels)-1, running_total[-1]), 
            xytext=(len(labels)-1, running_total[-1]+20),
            arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', shrink=0.05), ha='center')

# Labels and title
ax.set_xlabel('Year')
ax.set_ylabel('Wholesale Sales (million U.S. dollars)')
ax.set_title('Waterfall Chart of Incremental Wholesale Sales Changes (2007-2020)')
ax.grid(True, axis='y')

plt.xticks(rotation=45)

arrivals = np.array([1.34, 1.55, 1.59, 1.25, 1.36, 1.58, 2.24, 2.46, 2.67, 2.81, 3.06, 3.25, 3.62])

arrivals = arrivals[mask]

# Normalize years
x = years - years[0]

# Define models
def linear_model(x, a, b):
    return a * x + b

def exponential_model(x, a, b):
    return b * np.exp(a * x)

def quadratic_model(x, a, b, c):
    return a * x**2 + b * x + c

popt_exp, _ = curve_fit(exponential_model, x, arrivals, p0=(0.1, 1))

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import pandas as pd

# Data
years = [2005, 2006, 2007, 2008, 2009, 2010,
         2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016,
         2017, 2018, 2019, 2020]

expenditures = [44643, 45731, 44977, 45432, 43071, 41796,
                42476, 43561, 45149, 47928, 49463, 49829,
                50834, 50955, 50613, 37930]

# Create a dataframe
data = pd.DataFrame({'Year': years, 'Expenditure': expenditures})

# Calculate year-over-year percentage changes
data['YoY_Percent_Change'] = data['Expenditure'].pct_change() * 100

# Calculate average percentage change
average_pct_change = data['YoY_Percent_Change'][1:].mean()

# Deviation from average
data['Deviation'] = data['YoY_Percent_Change'] - average_pct_change

# Find the year with largest negative deviation
idx_max_neg_deviation = data['Deviation'].idxmin()
max_negative_deviation_year = data.loc[idx_max_neg_deviation, 'Year']

# Plot
plt.figure(figsize=(12, 6))
plt.scatter(data['Year'], data['Expenditure'], color='blue')

# Highlight the year with largest negative deviation
plt.scatter(max_negative_deviation_year, data.loc[idx_max_neg_deviation, 'Expenditure'], color='red', s=100, label='Largest Negative Deviation')

plt.xlabel('Year')
plt.ylabel('Expenditure in million GBP')
plt.title("Annual Expenditure from 2005 to 2020")

# Annotate key insights
plt.legend()

plt.annotate(f'Year with largest negative deviation: {int(max_negative_deviation_year)}',
             xy=(max_negative_deviation_year, data.loc[idx_max_neg_deviation, 'Expenditure']),
             xytext=(max_negative_deviation_year + 1, data['Expenditure'].min() + 5000),
             arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', arrowstyle='->'))

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd

years = [2015.0, 2020.0, 2025.0, 2030.0, 2035.0, 2040.0, 2045.0, 2050.0, 2055.0, 2060.0]
population = [321.37, 334.5, 347.34, 359.4, 370.34, 380.22, 389.39, 398.33, 407.41, 416.8]

df = pd.DataFrame({'Year': years, 'Population': population})

# Calculate annual growth rates for each 5-year interval
annual_growth_rates = []
growth_years = []
for i in range(len(df)-1):
    start_pop = df.iloc[i]['Population']
    end_pop = df.iloc[i+1]['Population']
    growth_rate = ((end_pop / start_pop)**(1/5) - 1) * 100
    annual_growth_rates.append(growth_rate)
    mid_year = (df.iloc[i]['Year'] + df.iloc[i+1]['Year']) / 2
    growth_years.append(mid_year)

# Calculate population increases per interval
population_increases = [df['Population'].iloc[i+1] - df['Population'].iloc[i] for i in range(len(df)-1)]

# Create subplots
fig, axs = plt.subplots(3, 1, figsize=(10, 15))

# Plot population over time
axs[0].plot(df['Year'], df['Population'], marker='o')
axs[0].set_title('Population over Time')
axs[0].set_xlabel('Year')
axs[0].set_ylabel('Population (millions)')
axs[0].annotate('Total Increase: {:.2f} million'.format(df['Population'].iloc[-1] - df['Population'].iloc[0]),
                xy=(df['Year'].iloc[-1], df['Population'].iloc[-1]),
                xytext=(df['Year'].iloc[-1]-20, df['Population'].iloc[-1]-20),
                arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', arrowstyle='->'))

# Plot annual growth rates over time
axs[1].bar(growth_years, annual_growth_rates, width=4)
axs[1].set_title('Annual Growth Rates over Time')
axs[1].set_xlabel('Year')
axs[1].set_ylabel('Annual Growth Rate (%)')
axs[1].set_ylim(0, max(annual_growth_rates)*1.1)
axs[1].annotate('Avg Growth Rate: {:.2f}%'.format(np.mean(annual_growth_rates)),
                xy=(growth_years[len(growth_years)//2], annual_growth_rates[len(annual_growth_rates)//2]),
                xytext=(growth_years[len(growth_years)//2]-15, max(annual_growth_rates)*0.9),
                arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', arrowstyle='->'))

# Plot population increase per interval
axs[2].bar(growth_years, population_increases, width=4)
axs[2].set_title('5-Year Population Increase')
axs[2].set_xlabel('Year')
axs[2].set_ylabel('Population Increase (millions)')
axs[2].annotate('Max Increase: {:.2f} million'.format(max(population_increases)),
                xy=(growth_years[np.argmax(population_increases)], max(population_increases)),
                xytext=(growth_years[np.argmax(population_increases)]-15, max(population_increases)*0.9),
                arrowprops=dict(facecolor='black', arrowstyle='->'))

plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
